title: orobanche major subsp. icterica
intro: Orobanche major subsp. icterica, also known as the yellow-blooming broomrape, is a parasitic flowering plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching to the roots of host plants, particularly those in the genus Thymus, such as thyme and marjoram. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Orobanche major subsp. icterica include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Orobanche major subsp. icterica can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
